stated_strategic_priorities: Scale evergreen program AUM to become the dominant private-markets vehicle format, targeting >50% of total AUM in open-ended structures
Expand private wealth distribution globally through bank and wealth-manager partnerships to capture the retail-to-alternatives rotation
Deepen direct investing capabilities across all asset classes to control deal origination, value creation, and exit timing without reliance on third-party GPs

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: Global alternative AUM projected at ~$25T by 2028 (Preqin). Partners Group's $174B AUM represents <1% share. The firm's strategic positioning around evergreen products targets the estimated $60T+ global individual investor wealth pool that remains largely unallocated to private markets. The private wealth channel is expected to grow from ~16% to ~25% of alt fundraising by 2027.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Swiss-listed corporation with single share class and broadly dispersed institutional shareholder base. Co-founders (including executive chairman Steffen Meister until 2024) have transitioned toward professional management. David Layton serves as CEO (appointed 2019). No dual-class structure; no controlling shareholder. Partners Group employees collectively hold a significant minority stake, aligning management incentives. Board includes independent directors with institutional investment backgrounds.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: Regulated by FINMA (Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority) as a Swiss asset manager. European fund vehicles subject to AIFMD and AIFMD II (effective 2026). US-registered investment adviser with SEC. Evergreen product expansion depends on favorable regulatory treatment of semi-liquid structures across jurisdictions, particularly ELTIF 2.0 in Europe (effective January 2024) which broadened retail access to long-term investment funds.
